what_it_does: Defines the React UI (AcIngest) for the ingestion flow: a rail with a drop/paste zone and a source list, and a detail pane that shows a selected source, lets the user trigger deep analysis, and displays the resulting design-DNA findings grouped by dimension with per-finding and bulk 'bring forward' actions. All model/data work is delegated to the CV_SOURCE registry.
observations: Subscribes to CV_SOURCE.on(force) so the UI re-renders whenever the source corpus changes.
DIM maps dimensions (color/type/voice/structure/value/motif) to labels and icons; TYPE_LABEL maps recognizer types to display labels.
onFiles reads each file via FileReader (DataURL for images, text otherwise), calls CV_SOURCE.addSource then CV_SOURCE.recognize, and selects it.
onPaste creates a timestamped pasted-note source and recognizes it.
Dropzone has Upload/Paste tabs, drag-over state, a hidden file input, and a paste textarea with an Add button.
SourceView triggers CV_SOURCE.analyze with busy/error state, renders findings grouped by dimension, and offers per-finding 'Bring forward' and a 'Bring all N forward' button.
Color findings whose evidence is a hex string render a live swatch; evidence is shown as a quote.
Uses window.dsaToast for staging feedback and shows a loud inline error block on analyze failure.
Reads StateDot/AcKit off window and uses CvIcon/ViShape globals.
standouts: Findings are presented as reviewable, dimension-grouped cards with evidence quotes and explicit bring-forward actions — keeping the human in the loop before anything is staged.
Image sources show a thumbnail and color findings render actual swatches, tying evidence to the visual.
Errors from analysis are surfaced inline rather than swallowed, matching the loud-fail ethos.
conventions: Pure projection of the CV_SOURCE registry — the UI reads list/get and calls registry methods, holding no parallel state, consistent with 'interface is a projection of the registries'.
window-global components (AcKit, CvIcon, ViShape) and React-off-window, consistent with the project's no-import style.
Reuses fz2-btn classes from the Foundations stylesheet for buttons (shared styling).
concerns: FileReader onerror just resolves the promise (res) with no user-visible error, so a failed file read is silent.
onFiles calls recognize but not analyze; recognize is fire-and-forget (no await), so ordering relative to the re-render relies on the subscription.
Borrows fz2-btn class names from Foundations, coupling this view's styling to another file's CSS.
notes: The ingest UI: drop/paste sources into a rail, pick one, click 'Analyse for depth', then review dimension-grouped DNA findings and bring chosen proposals forward into the system.
questions: Should a failed FileReader read surface an error to the user rather than silently resolving?
Where are the fz2-btn styles defined and is reusing them across views an intentional shared convention?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header promises an upload→recognize→deep-read→reviewable-proposals UI composed from AcKit with all model work via CV_SOURCE, and the component delivers exactly that interaction.
apparent_themes: UI as a thin projection of a registry
intention_signals: Trying to give the user a simple, trustworthy surface to feed inspiration in, watch Vi recognize and read it, and decide which extracted design signals to stage into their system.
novelty: Presenting model-extracted design DNA as dimension-grouped, evidence-backed cards with granular and bulk staging — analysis output rendered as a review queue rather than freeform text.
contribution: Contributes the ingestion user interface: the drop/paste capture, the source list with recognition status, and the findings review pane with per-finding and bulk bring-forward staging that sits atop the CV_SOURCE registry.
summary_for_embedding: A React ingest UI for AtomiCity that uploads or pastes source material, shows a source list with recognition status, triggers deep analysis via CV_SOURCE, and renders design-DNA findings grouped by dimension with bring-forward staging actions.
intention_for_embedding: To give the user a trustworthy surface to feed in inspiration, watch Vi recognize and deeply read it, and review and stage the extracted design signals into their system.
